seoTitle: How to Link a Google Domain to Gumroad After Migration
metaDescription: Since Google Domains moved to Squarespace, learn how to find your domain there and connect it to Gumroad with the correct CNAME record.
intro: Google Domains has migrated to Squarespace Domains, so connecting a domain you originally registered there now means working through Squarespace's DNS panel instead. This guide covers entering your domain into Gumroad's advanced settings and setting up the CNAME record on the Squarespace side so everything points correctly.

steps.heading: Open Gumroad's advanced settings
steps.body: Go to your Gumroad settings and find the advanced section where custom domain entry lives.

steps.heading: Enter your target domain
steps.body: Type your root domain or subdomain into the custom domain field and save it.

steps.heading: Log into Squarespace Domains
steps.body: Since Google Domains migrated, find your domain inventory inside your Squarespace account instead.

steps.heading: Add a CNAME record
steps.body: Create a new CNAME record pointing your domain to Gumroad's target endpoint.

steps.heading: Confirm the connection
steps.body: Check the domain status inside Gumroad to confirm it's verified after DNS propagation.

faq.question: Why do I need to use Squarespace instead of Google Domains?
faq.answer: Google officially migrated Google Domains accounts to Squarespace Domains, so DNS management now happens there.

faq.question: What record type connects my domain to Gumroad?
faq.answer: You'll create a CNAME record pointing to Gumroad's target endpoint.

faq.question: How long does it take for the domain to activate?
faq.answer: DNS changes can take time to propagate, so the connection may not appear verified immediately.

faq.question: Can I still use my old Google Domains login?
faq.answer: Since the migration, domain management happens through your Squarespace account instead.
